data.title: Esperion settles patent dispute with Dr. Reddy’s over cholesterol drugs
data.description:
• Esperion Therapeutics (NASDAQ:ESPR) announced it has reached a settlement with Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories (NYSE:RDY) regarding the latter’s bid to launch generic versions of NEXLETOL and NEXLIZET in the U.S.
  • Under the agreement, Dr. Reddy’s will not market generic versions of the two cholesterol-lowering drugs before April 19, 2040, when the relevant patents expire.
  • Esperion noted that patent litigation with other defendants over the same products remains ongoing.

data.title: OpenAI’s new Sora app tops App Store
data.description:
OpenAI’s newly launched app Sora has secured the top spot in Apple’s App Store ahead of Google’s (GOOG) Gemini and the Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) startup’s own ChatGPT.

The video editing and creation app, which was launched on Tuesday, is powered by OpenAI's Sora 2 video generation model, which lets users input text, image and video to create video.

Despite being invite-only for now and limited to users in the U.S. and Canada, Sora reportedly saw 56,000 downloads on its first day on the U.S. App Store.

“It’s been epic to see what the collective creativity of humanity is capable of so far,” Bill Peebles, head of Sora at OpenAI, wrote in a post on X on Friday. “Team is iterating fast and listening to feedback.”

ChatGPT Pro users will be able to access the company's higher quality Sora 2 Pro model on sora.com and soon in the Sora app. There will also be a Sora 2 API coming, OpenAI said, adding that it plans to quickly expand the app to additional countries.

data.title: Oracle's consensus estimates for cloud capex remain too low: KeyBanc
data.description:
KeyBanc analysts believe the consensus estimates for Oracle's (NYSE:ORCL) cloud-related capital expenditures are too low, as the conversion rate for cloud capex to next-year revenue has significantly declined in recent years for all major cloud providers.

However, KeyBanc predicts that Oracle's cloud capex conversion rate is on path to improve in the years ahead and will be much stronger than Microsoft (MSFT) Azure and Amazon Web Services (AMZN).

"During the CPU and virtual machine days of the public cloud, which really took off about a decade ago, a dollar of capex in year 0 led to about 75 cents of incremental cloud revenue for Azure and AWS in year 1," said KeyBanc analysts Jackson Ader and Jack Nichols in an investor note. "As the CPU cloud has given way to the GPU cloud, the conversion of a dollar of capex has slipped from 86.9% in CY21 to an average of 49.6% from CY22-CY25."

For example, the cloud capex conversion for Azure and AWS in 2016 was 111% and 93%, respectively. The estimate for the current year has declined to 35% and 36%, respectively. The consensus estimate for Oracle for the entirety of calendar year 2025 is 47%.

"Cloud 2.0, the building of massive data centers filled with GPUs for a small number or even a single customer, shows a different story than the efficiency seen in Cloud 1.0," Ader added. "Conversion rates have come down meaningfully as businesses are now investing tens of billions of dollars into cloud infrastructure supporting the rise of AI technology and compute."

Over the next three years, the consensus calls for conversion rates of 42%, 63% and 97% for Oracle, respectively. In contrast, the estimates for Azure and AWS range from 30% to 34% for 2026 through 2028. However, KeyBanc's estimates for Oracle over the next three years are 28%, 39% and 70%, respectively. These are much stronger than AWS and Azure but still well below the consensus.

The difference in the percentages relates to KeyBanc estimating larger cloud capex than the market consensus. For example, KeyBanc projects Oracle's cloud capex in 2026 to reach $42.9B versus the consensus of $36.4B. KeyBanc believes this is due to other analysts not adequately reflecting lease commitments.

"Oracle went from zero finance leases at the end of its fiscal 2024 to over $4B of finance lease obligations at the end of its fiscal 2025," KeyBanc noted. "There is also over $100B in total lease commitments (both operating and finance) waiting to come on the balance sheet between FY26 and FY28."

KeyBanc retains its Overweight rating and $350 price target on Oracle.

data.title: Clariant rejects €2B damage claims by BP, Exxon
data.description:
Swiss specialty chemicals maker Clariant (OTCPK:CLZNF) (OTCPK:CLZNY) said Friday it rejects allegations from BP (NYSE:BP) and Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) that its purchases of ethylene broke European Union competition law.

Clariant (OTCPK:CLZNF) (OTCPK:CLZNY) said it is one of four companies that received claims for damages in lawsuits filed in Germany, including claims totaling €1.1 billion from BP (NYSE:BP) and €860 million from Exxon (NYSE:XOM).

Clariant (OTCPK:CLZNF) (OTCPK:CLZNY) said it rejects the allegations and will defend itself in court, adding that it has economic evidence that the conduct by the company and the other parties included in the lawsuits did not produce any effect on the ethylene market.

The company said previously that chemical company LyondellBasell (LYB) was seeking ~€1.6 billion in damages after alleging its purchases of ethylene broke E.U. competition law.

data.title: Wall Street edges higher as investors brush aside shutdown worries
data.description:
Wall Street’s broader market averages traded in the green on Friday as market participants shrug off concerns about the U.S. government shutdown for the third day.

Pushing higher was the blue-chip Dow (DJI) as it had climbed up by 0.6%. Also tracking higher on Friday was the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) as it advanced by 0.2%. The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) treads near even. 

On a sector-by-sector stance, eight of the 11 S&P sectors have traded higher, as Health Care leads the way. In reverse, the Communication Services segment has struggled the most and is the weakest sector on the session. 

U.S. Treasury yields ticked higher as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up 2 basis points to 4.11% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed up 2 basis points to 3.56%.

The U.S. government shutdown began at midnight Wednesday after Republicans and Democrats failed to agree on a federal funding deal. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC on Thursday that the shutdown could weigh on GDP growth.

So far, Wall Street has largely brushed off the impasse. On prediction market Kalshi, the probability of the shutdown lasting more than 10 days rose to 69%.

With federal agencies closed, investors will be left without the September nonfarm payrolls report—a release widely viewed as critical for the Federal Reserve’s assessment of labor market conditions.

In other economic news, S&P Global US Services PMI business activity index came in at 54.2 in September, down from 54.5 in August.

Under the magnificent seven umbrella, shares of Apple (AAPL) were lower after Jefferies downgraded the stock to Underperform from Hold citing excessive expectations on iPhones and foldable phones.

data.title: Robinhood, Invesco among top S&P 500 financial movers in Q3
data.description:
The Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLF), which tracks the S&P 500 financials sector, rose by 2.86% in the third quarter, running behind the 7.79% surge in the broader S&P500 index.

On a year-to-date basis, XLF trailed the broader markets. It advanced 10.4% compared to the S&P500’s 14.2% gain.

Industries Q3 Performance

The financials-focused ETF had net inflows of $2.83B in the third quarter.

Banks saw the biggest rise in the sector which advanced 8.9%, and financial services followed next, rising 1.32%. However, insurance was the only industry that lagged at -1.20%.

Top 5 movers in Q2

Gainers

  • Robinhood Markets (HOOD) +44.4%.
  • Invesco (IVZ) +37.9%.
  • The Bank of New York Mellon (BK) +20.2%.
  • Globe Life (GL) +18.7%.
  • Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR) +16.9%.

Losers

  • FactSet Research Systems (FDS) -35.9%.
  • Brown & Brown (BRO) -13.9%. The insurance broker extended its losses after shedding 12% in the last quarter. 
  • PayPal Holdings (PYPL) -12.6%.
  • Ameriprise Financial (AMP) -10.1%.
  • American International Group (AIG) -8.5%. 

What Analysts Expect

The Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF is upgraded from hold to buy, supported by strong fundamentals and technical momentum, said Seeking Alpha analyst Mike Zaccardi. The analyst added that XLF trades near all-time highs, with a rising 200-day moving average and an upside target of $63 in play.

What Quantitative Measures Say

XLF received a Hold rating from the SA quant system with a 3.15 score out of 5. This comes in large part due to a C grade in the category of risk. The stock, however, received high grades in other areas, with an B- for momentum, an A- for dividends and an A+ for liquidity and expenses.

data.title: ISM Services PMI unexpectedly slips to 50.0 in September
data.description:
The U.S. ISM Services Index came in at 50.0 in September from 52.0 in August, below the 51.6 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Thursday.

The services PMI reading of 50 percent was at the breakeven point between expansion and contraction for the first time since January 2010.

"September's Services PMI level returned to numbers very similar to May and July, with weakness in business activity and continued weakness in employment. Ten industries reported growth in September, two fewer than in August, while the number reporting contraction increased from four to seven," Steve Miller, chair of the ISM Services Business Survey Committee.

Business activity/production: 49.9 in September vs. 55.0 in the previous month.

Employment: 47.2 vs. 46.6 consensus and 46.5 in August.

Prices: 69.4 vs. 68.9 expected and 69.2 in August.

New orders: 50.4 vs. 56.0 prior.

data.title: U.S. Composite PMI slips in September amid weaker output expansions
data.description:
S&P Global US Services PMI business activity index recorded 54.2 in September, down from 54.5 in August. The final US Services PMI is higher from 53.9 flash figure, but has fallen for two months in a row, representing a slowdown from July’s year-to-date peak. 

Business confidence improved in September, strengthening to its highest since May. There are some uncertainty in the outlook, especially around tariffs, lower interest rates in some instances were reported to have boosted optimism by adding to hopes of a pickup of demand in the year ahead. Several panelists also linked their positive sentiment to expectations that federal government policies will support economic growth in the year ahead.

The S&P Global US Composite PMI recorded 53.9 in September, down from 54.6 in August and represented the slowest growth for three months. Both sectors covered by the survey recorded weaker output expansions in line with slowergains in new business.

Employment meanwhile barely rose, but confidence in the outlook strengthened noticeably. Cost pressures remained elevated, although inflation softened to a five-month low. A similar trend was seen for output charges.

data.title: Canada's composite PMI contracts in September as private sector output weighs
data.description:
Canada's September final service PMI came in at 46.3, much below the forecast of 49.0. September figures were lower than the August service figure of 48.6.

Activity continued to fall, whilst spare capacity and a lack of new business led to a decline in employment. Cost pressures also remained present, amid reports ofhigher labour and fuel expenses.  

September global composite PMI of 46.3 trailed the consensus of 49.0, indicating a contracting economy. Falling to 46.3, down from 48.4 in August, the index pointed to an accelerated and marked contraction in private sector output.    

Readings above 50.0 signal an improvement in business activity on the previous month while those below 50.0 show deterioration.

data.title: Groq plans to set up over 12 data centers next year: report
data.description:
Groq's CEO Jonathan Ross said the company intends to set up over a dozen data centers next year, the Wall Street Journal reported.

Groq, which develops hardware made specifically for AI inference, was last valued at $6.9B following a $750M funding round that was led by Disruptive including investments from BlackRock, Neuberger Berman, Deutsche Telekom Capital Partners, Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) and Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO).

So far this year, the American company has established 12 data centers, and next year it wants to exceed that number, according to Ross, the report noted.

“Expect that [number] to grow rapidly,” Ross, who founded the company, said in an interview, the report added.

Groq did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Groq has data centers in the U.S., Canada, the Middle East, and Europe, and intends to establish its first in Asia this year, as per Ross who previously headed Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) unit Google’s AI chip-development program.

Asia is a vital market, about half of the developers that use Groq’s technology come from the region, the report added.

“One reason that we’ve done so well in all of Asia, but in particular in India, is it’s a very price-sensitive market," said Ross.

Groq builds custom hardware known as Language Processing Units, or LPUs, and a full-stack platform called GroqCloud. These are designed to accelerate AI inference and create new use cases for large language models and other AI applications.

AI inference is the ability of trained AI models to recognize patterns and draw conclusions from new information that they have not seen before.

Groq has deployed chips to Saudi Arabia, where it secured a $1.5B deal earlier this year to expand the delivery of its infrastructure. The demand there is already surpassing supply, as per Ross.

“Every time we overcompensate by trying to build more, people surprise us by needing even more than we built,” said Ross.

As companies look to achieve self-sufficiency in AI, Ross anticipates many to try to build their own chips. However, he thinks that the odds are stacked against them.

“The failure rate’s…probably 9 out of 10 or higher despite [these companies] spending billions and billions of dollars,” said Ross.

In addition, when asked if Groq has received any acquisition offers, Ross indicated that he has no plan of selling the company at the moment, nor has he sold any of his Groq shares, the report added.
